title: VibeTeacher Core

test_results.input: This looks like those sort of non-overlapping worker assignments
test_results.result: Successfully detected MECE decomposition teaching moment
Moment stored with id tm-1ea14d40, acknowledged/absorbed flags working
get_pending_moments() correctly filters by acknowledged flag

artifacts.description: Technical vocabulary storage with 10 pre-populated Pulse terms. Schema: term, definition, v_description, precise_term, context, first_encountered, absorbed, usage_count
Generate teaching moments using glossary matching (detect_imprecision) and LLM analysis (generate_moment_with_llm). Handles term tracking, absorption, and usage counts.
Orchestrate VibeTeacher at HITL checkpoints. Non-blocking moment storage to teaching_moments.jsonl. CLI with activate, pending, acknowledge, sms-summary commands.

success_criteria.criterion: Glossary schema supports all required fields
success_criteria.notes: All required fields present: term, definition, v_description, precise_term, context, first_encountered, absorbed, usage_count
detect_imprecision() uses substring matching and word overlap (40% threshold). Test: 'non-overlapping' -> 'MECE decomposition' ✓
activate_at_checkpoint() stores moments without requiring acknowledgement. Returns moment for optional display, build continues.
teaching_moments.jsonl stores each moment with acknowledged/absorbed flags. mark_absorbed() updates glossary term.absorbed = True
activate_at_checkpoint() ready for integration with seeded_judge.py, review_manager.py, feedback_extractor.py. D6.2 will implement hooks.

implementation_notes: No existing VibeTeacher implementation found. Created from scratch in N5/pulse/teaching/
Glossary pre-populated with 10 Pulse-specific terms: MECE, Drop, Stream, Deposit, Brief, Seeded, HITL, Orchestrator, Current, Pulse
Teaching moments stored build-local at N5/builds/<slug>/teaching_moments.jsonl (one JSONL per build)
Glossary matching uses substring matching first, then word overlap for fuzzy detection
Absorption tracking allows reducing repeat explanations once V demonstrates understanding
CLI tested: activate, pending, acknowledge, sms-summary commands working correctly
Integration documented in D6.1 brief - hooks ready for D6.2 implementation

integration_readiness.seeded_judge.py: Ready - call activate_at_checkpoint('interview_complete', task_id, synthesis.get('combined_text')) after marking seeded
integration_readiness.review_manager.py: Ready - call activate_at_checkpoint('plan_review', slug, response) when V responds in process_review_response()
integration_readiness.feedback_extractor.py: Ready - call activate_at_checkpoint('feedback', slug, feedback) when extracting learnings
integration_readiness.build_close: Ready - get_pending_for_sms() can be called at build end for digest

known_limitations: Glossary matching is simple (substring + word overlap), may need LLM fallback for complex patterns
Finding moments across all builds (acknowledge_moment) iterates all build directories - inefficient for large numbers
LLM analysis (generate_moment_with_llm) stubbed - would use Zo API for novel pattern detection
No init.py to prevent relative import conflicts - direct imports required
learnings: Separate glossary.json from SYSTEM_LEARNINGS.json - different purposes (vocabulary vs process)
Pre-populating glossary with domain-specific terms accelerates teaching from day 1
Teaching moments must be non-blocking to avoid disrupting build flow
Absorption tracking reduces repeat explanations once V demonstrates understanding
CLI testing with real inputs critical for validation (found import issues through testing)
Avoid __init__.py in multi-file teaching package to prevent relative import conflicts
